title: input-form widget params
description: A shared widget module for the input-calculator and input-plot families: typed-entry fields (number or text) drive a bespoke per-widget computation whose output lands in a .readout div (input-calculator) or in a .readout div plus an SVG canvas (input-plot). The schema is single-mode with optional plot fields; the svg* block activates the input-plot branch. The computation logic is passed through as a `bodyScript` artifact string that renderScript drops into the IIFE — portable consumers (React, etc.) may re-implement the compute from scratch or evaluate the script in a sandbox.

widgetId.description: DOM id for the outer <div class="widget"> wrapper.

titleTag.description: Tag used for the .ttl header element inside .hd. Default 'div' emits <div class="ttl">; 'span' emits <span class="ttl"> (a layout sub-variant in some legacy topics).

hintTag.description: Tag used for the .hint header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' pairs with titleTag='span' in the span-header layout sub-variant.

inputs.description: Ordered list of input fields inside the single .row. Each entry may carry a leading <label> (rendered before the input) and/or a trailing <label> (rendered after the input).

label.description: Optional leading <label for=id>…</label>. Omit to suppress.

labelWraps.description: When true and `label` is present, the <input> nests INSIDE the <label> as `<label>labelText <input ...></label>` (the `for=` attr is omitted). When false/absent, emits the default sibling form `<label for=id>labelText</label><input ...>`.

value.description: Raw value= attribute (string; numeric widgets still use string to preserve bytes like "2" vs "2.0").

for.description: `for=` target id (may point at this input or an adjacent one).

text.description: Label text content (may include KaTeX-wrapped math).

trailingLabel.description: Optional <label for=…>…</label> rendered immediately AFTER this input, inside the same .row.

button.description: The action button that triggers the computation.

readoutId.description: DOM id for the <div class="readout"> where the computation's textual output is written.

readoutPrefix.description: Optional prefix HTML rendered inside the readout div at markup time. Most widgets leave it empty and let the IIFE populate the readout.

svgId.description: Optional: DOM id for an <svg> canvas rendered BEFORE the .row. Presence switches the widget into the input-plot branch.

viewBox.description: Required iff svgId is set. Raw viewBox attr value (e.g. "0 0 620 160").

svgWidthAttr.description: Optional raw width= attr for the <svg> (e.g. "620" or "100%").

svgHeightAttr.description: Optional raw height= attr for the <svg>.

svgStyleAttr.description: Optional raw style attr for the <svg>.

bodyScript.description: ARTIFACT. The verbatim IIFE body (everything between `(function(){` and `})();`) that wires up the button, reads inputs, and writes the readout / SVG. Byte-preserved so renderScript can round-trip the original inline source exactly. A portable frontend (React, etc.) ignores this field and implements compute(inputs) → readout on its own.

trailingExplainer.description:
Optional

...

rendered after the readout. Inner HTML.

hdHtml.description:
ARTIFACT. When present, overrides the default
...
line builder; the renderer emits `
${hdHtml}
\n` verbatim. Use for multi-line .hd variants that the default titleTag/hintTag + one-line template can't reproduce.
